nina.name: Na (Nina) Hu
nina.qualifications:
• Bachelor of Musculoskeletal Health
  • Postgraduate Diploma in Osteopathy
  • Postgraduate Certificate in Health Science (Acupuncture)
  • Paediatric Osteopathy
  • Neural Neuromeningeal Manipulation
nina.approach: Nina integrates multiple modalities into each session at no extra charge. Initial consultations (45–60 minutes) include a full case history, physical examination, and osteopathic hands-on treatment and acupuncture where appropriate. Follow-up sessions are tailored to each patient's response and may include dry needling, auricular or electrical acupuncture, cupping, ultrasound therapy, and moxibustion, alongside personalised rehabilitation to support lasting recovery.
nina.specialInterests: Nina has a special interest in women's health, integrating osteopathic cranial techniques and acupuncture to support hormonal balance, pelvic health, and overall wellbeing. She has specialist training in Paediatric Osteopathy, making her well placed to care for infants and young children with great gentleness and sensitivity.
nina.aboutMe: Nina's journey into osteopathy and acupuncture began in childhood, when her grandparents introduced her to Traditional Chinese Medicine, energy healing, naturopathy, and acupuncture — sparking a lifelong fascination with the body's innate capacity for healing. Though verbal expression was once a challenge, she discovered an exceptional gift for intuitive feeling and palpation that continues to define her clinical style. Outside the clinic, Nina practises meditation and qigong, and can often be found on the badminton court, on the ski slopes, or out on the water kayaking.

jonathan.name: Jonathan Grice

credentials.name: Diploma in Osteopathy, British College of Osteopathic Medicine

jonathan.qualifications: Jonathan trained at the British College of Osteopathic Medicine in London and is a NZ Registered Osteopath. After graduating, he built extensive clinical experience in private practice from 2010, before holding an NHS contract in Twickenham where he received referrals from local general practice. From 2019 to 2022 he worked in advanced clinical practice at an NHS general practice, focusing on musculoskeletal diagnosis and onward referral. Born and raised in Christchurch, Jonathan returned home in 2022 — bringing with him over a decade of international experience, including a period as a vaccinator during the COVID-19 pandemic.
jonathan.approach: Jonathan's treatments draw on structural, classical, and cranial osteopathic techniques, tailored to each patient's presentation and goals. Alongside hands-on osteopathy, he incorporates bio-photomodulation (low-level laser therapy) for pain management and tissue healing — an area in which he has developed particular expertise through his work with THOR Photomedicine, where he authored over 200 treatment protocols. He also uses kinesiology taping and exercise rehabilitation where clinically appropriate.
jonathan.specialInterests: Jonathan has a deep specialist interest in bio-photomodulation and its clinical applications. His work with THOR Photomedicine has seen him develop protocols for a wide range of presentations — from aphthous ulcers to post-herpetic neuralgia. He also brings extensive clinical experience from his years working within NHS general practice.
jonathan.aboutMe: Born and raised in Christchurch, Jonathan spent many years working in the UK before returning home in 2022. He is glad to be back in the city he grew up in, and brings a broad clinical perspective shaped by years of NHS practice and international collaboration. Outside the clinic, Jonathan remains connected to his community and enjoys the outdoor lifestyle that Christchurch and the surrounding region have to offer.

consultationSteps.title: Initial Consultation
consultationSteps.description: Allow 40 minutes for a full case history, physical examination, and treatment plan discussion. Includes osteopathic hands-on treatment and low level laser therapy (bio-photomodulation) where appropriate — all at no extra charge.

consultationSteps.title: Follow-up Treatments
consultationSteps.description: Tailored to your response and progress.

consultationSteps.title: Rehabilitation
consultationSteps.description: Personalised rehabilitation to support lasting recovery and help prevent future injuries.

maddison.name: Maddison Moffat

credentials.name: Bachelor of Musculoskeletal Health, Ara Institute of Canterbury
Postgraduate Diploma in Osteopathy, Ara Institute of Canterbury

maddison.qualifications: Maddison completed a Bachelor of Musculoskeletal Health and a Postgraduate Diploma in Osteopathy at Ara Institute of Canterbury. She is registered with the Osteopathic Council of New Zealand, and is an ACC-registered provider. Born and raised in Christchurch, Maddison developed a passion for understanding the human body and movement from an early age through her lifelong involvement in sport.
maddison.approach: Maddison embraces osteopathy's holistic philosophy, providing individualised, patient-centred care that considers the whole body rather than just the site of pain or injury. She works with patients across a broad spectrum — from acute injuries to long-standing chronic conditions — adapting her approach to suit each person's needs, history, and goals. Her aim is always to help people return to the activities they love.
maddison.specialInterests: Maddison's clinical interests are informed by her own active lifestyle and lifelong connection to sport. She also brings genuine enthusiasm to working with younger patients, with care and patience adapted to each child. Whether supporting an athlete through recovery or helping a patient manage ongoing pain, Maddison brings the same commitment and warmth to every consultation.
maddison.aboutMe: Outside the clinic, Maddison lives an active life — you'll find her at the gym, out walking, or cheering on her favourite sports teams. A proud Christchurch local, she loves being part of a community-focused practice where she can make a real difference to people's everyday lives. She believes that a strong therapeutic relationship is the foundation of good care, and works hard to make every patient feel genuinely supported.

consultationSteps.title: Initial Consultation
consultationSteps.description: Allow 45–60 minutes for a full case history, physical examination, and treatment plan discussion. Includes osteopathic hands-on treatment.

consultationSteps.title: Follow-up Treatments
consultationSteps.description: Tailored to your response and progress.

consultationSteps.title: Rehabilitation
consultationSteps.description: Personalised rehabilitation to support lasting recovery and help prevent future injuries.

min.name: Min Jin

registration.name: Chinese Medicine Council of New Zealand

credentials.name: Bachelor of Clinical Medicine, Gansu University of Chinese Medicine
Master of Biomedical Project Management, University of Science and Technology of China
Bachelor of Acupuncture and Traditional Chinese Medicine, New Zealand School of Acupuncture and Traditional Chinese Medicine

min.qualifications: Min holds a Bachelor of Clinical Medicine from Gansu University of Chinese Medicine (2002) and a Master of Biomedical Project Management from the University of Science and Technology of China (2013). In 2025, she completed a Bachelor's degree at the New Zealand School of Acupuncture and Traditional Chinese Medicine, which included over 320 hours of supervised clinical practice. Min is a registered member of the Chinese Medicine Council of New Zealand (CMCNZ). Before establishing her acupuncture practice in New Zealand, Min spent many years in clinical research — including four years at Peking University Stem Cell Research Centre and over a decade managing nationally approved clinical projects in areas such as Parkinson's disease, spinal cord injury, and oncology.
min.approach: Min provides safe, evidence-informed, and patient-centred care, weaving together classical Chinese medicine theory with Tung-style acupuncture techniques. She takes time to understand each patient's full picture — physical, emotional, and lifestyle — before forming a treatment plan. Alongside acupuncture, she draws on moxibustion, cupping, and therapeutic massage where clinically appropriate, tailoring every session to the individual.
min.specialInterests: Min's background in clinical research gives her a deep appreciation for evidence-based practice, which she brings together with the wisdom of traditional Chinese medicine to offer a uniquely informed approach to care.
min.aboutMe: Min's path to acupuncture is shaped by a rich background in both science and medicine. Her years in clinical research gave her a profound respect for evidence-based thinking, which she combines with a genuine belief in the healing potential of traditional Chinese medicine. Outside of work, Min values balance — she enjoys time in nature, nourishing food, and the quieter rhythms of everyday life. She brings patience, warmth, and genuine curiosity to every consultation.

consultationSteps.description: Allow 60 minutes for a full case history, physical examination, and treatment plan discussion.

consultationSteps.title: Follow-up Treatments
consultationSteps.description: Tailored to your response and progress. May include auricular acupuncture, cupping and moxibustion.

consultationSteps.description: Personalised rehabilitation to support lasting recovery and help prevent future injuries.
